---
title: "Machine Learning Summer School 2026"
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
    toc: false
---

::: {.hub-hero}
# Welcome

::: {.hub-tagline}
Slides, notebooks, and a short map of the week — all in one place.
:::

::: {.hub-lead}
Use this site **during and after class**: open today’s deck, then try the matching notebook when you feel like coding along. If something is unclear, that’s normal — bring it to the room or office hours. Separate lab exercises (if any) will be announced there.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip .hub-start appearance="simple"}
## Start here (2 minutes)

1. **Open today’s slides** — we follow **Monday → Tuesday → Thursday** in class.
2. **When you’re ready**, pick a notebook from the menu — same R you already use (RStudio or Positron is fine).
3. **Skim “Your week”** below for the story of the three days — no jargon quiz attached.
:::

::: {.hub-grid}
::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Palmer Penguins
:::
### Our real-data running example

- **[About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd)** — variables, biology, caveats, and how we reuse penguins **Monday → Thursday**  
- Links to **penguin-only** notebooks from that page (mass, binary species, sex, three-class species)

Synthetic **gene** notebooks stay the place for **known-truth** LASSO / ridge pedagogy.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Class slides
:::
### Follow along day by day

- **[Monday](slides/day-01-monday.qmd)** — from questions to simple models: when a straight line (or curve) helps, and when a model is “trying too hard.”  
- **[Tuesday](slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)** — branching decisions (trees), and how to keep your code organized so Thursday still makes sense.  
- **[Thursday](slides/day-03-thursday.qmd)** — richer models, what to measure when the world is messy, and how to talk about a prediction without overselling it.  

Each deck links back here from the **footer** if you get lost.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Hands-on notebooks
:::
### Try the code yourself

- **[Notebook home](notebooks/index.qmd)** — all notebooks + setup in plain steps  
- **Genes (known truth):** [linear + LASSO](notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic + tree](notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)  
- **Palmer Penguins:** [body mass regression](notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd), [Adelie vs Gentoo](notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd), [sex prediction](notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd), [three species](notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd)  

Go as fast or slow as you like; the notebooks are for **you**, not for a grade on this site.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Outline & modules
:::
### If you want more structure

- **[Curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd)** — how the three days connect (also useful if you miss a session)  
- **[Module library](modules/index.qmd)** — six draft modules (01–06): [Foundations](modules/index.qmd#module-01-foundations-and-generalization), [Regularization](modules/index.qmd#module-02-regularization), [Trees](modules/index.qmd#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures), [Tidymodels](modules/index.qmd#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline), [Advanced models](modules/index.qmd#module-05-advanced-models), [Metrics & SHAP](modules/index.qmd#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap)  

Your instructors may point you to extra readings or exercises; those live wherever the course announces them.
:::
:::

::: {.hub-week}
## Your week at a glance

::: {.hub-week-intro}
Think of it as one story in three chapters: **see the pattern**, **keep your work honest**, then **choose and explain** models without pretending they’re magic. Curiosity beats perfection.
:::

- **Monday** — predictions, when a model is “too wiggly,” and friendly introductions to linear models, logistic regression, and a bit of regularization. You’ll see **synthetic genes** (known truth) and **Palmer Penguins** (real measurements) in parallel.  
- **Tuesday** — decision trees and how we split the world into simple rules — plus habits that keep code readable when things get more complex.  
- **Thursday** — stronger models, what to watch when outcomes aren’t balanced, and how to discuss **why** a model leaned one way — without claiming it “knows” biology or medicine by itself.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## For instructors & TAs (technical)

- **Local preview:** from this project folder run `quarto preview` so links resolve; after `quarto render`, you can serve `_site/` with any static file server.  

- **Course design detail** — the [curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd) describes the two-track story (genes + Palmer Penguins), calibration, causal guardrails around explainability, data/model cards, reproducibility, and assessment prompts.  
- **Site output** lives in `_site/`; GitHub Actions builds the same way for Pages.  
- **Palmer Penguins** — [About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd); Tuesday `tidymodels` include uses the same **Adelie vs Gentoo** slice as the penguin classification notebook.  

- **Shared tidymodels include** — `_includes/tidymodels-pipeline.qmd` (slides + Module 04); link or excerpt in class as needed.
:::
